title: JSON subcolumn extraction returns NULL when defining nested Array(JSON) type
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
When creating a table with a JSON type column and explicitly defining a sub-column as Array(JSON), ClickHouse fails to extract key values from nested JSON objects.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.10.3
### How to reproduce
```sql
CREATE TABLE test (
json JSON(DeviceProperties Array(JSON))
) ENGINE = Memory;
INSERT INTO test VALUES ('{"DeviceProperties":[{"Name":"OS","Value":"Windows11"}]}');
select
json.DeviceProperties,
json.DeviceProperties[].Name
FROM test;
returns
['{"Name":"OS","Value":"Windows11"}']	[NULL]
```
### Expected behavior
returns
['{"Name":"OS","Value":"Windows11"}']	["OS"]
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
This suggests that when a nested JSON field is explicitly typed as Array(JSON), sub-key extraction (.Name) does not work correctly.
However, if the sub-column type is not specified and ClickHouse handles parsing automatically, everything works as expected.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (json JSON) ENGINE = MEMORY;
SELECT json, JSON_VALUE(json, '$.DeviceProperties[0].Name') AS Name FROM test1;
CREATE TABLE test2 (id INT, json JSON) ENGINE = MEMORY;
key_bug_features: Nested Array(JSON) type fails sub-key extraction
